faqs.question: Does a PNS X-Ray scan hurt?
faqs.answer: No. It’s completely painless and non-invasive.

faqs.question: How long does the test take?
faqs.answer: Total time at the centre: 10–15 minutes.

faqs.question: Is radiation safe for kids?
faqs.answer: Yes. Cadabams uses pediatric settings and lead shielding; dose is minimal.

faqs.question: Can I eat before the scan?
When will I get the report?
Do I need a doctor’s referral?
faqs.answer: Yes, to ensure the correct imaging protocol and insurance billing.

seo.title: PNS X-Ray Scan | Fast & Low-Dose Imaging at Cadabams
seo.description: Understand what a PNS X-Ray scan is, when you need it, risks, procedure and low-dose digital imaging benefits at Cadabams Diagnostics. Get same-day reports.

test.markdown:
## PNS X-Ray Scan Overview

- \*\*Layman’s definition\*\*: A simple head X-Ray focused on your sinus cavities to check for blockages, infection or swelling.
- \*\*Cadabams advantage\*\*: Same-day scheduling, low-dose digital detectors and expert radiologists reporting in under two hours.
---
## What is PNS X-Ray Scan?
### Medical Definition
A low-dose X-Ray examination that captures the \*\*paranasal sinus anatomy\*\*—the frontal, maxillary, ethmoid and sphenoid sinuses.
### PNS View vs. Standard Skull X-Ray
| Feature | PNS View | Standard Skull |
|---------|----------|----------------|
| Area focused | Sinuses only | Entire cranium |
| Radiation dose | ~0.03 mSv | ~0.1 mSv |
| Detail for sinusitis | High | Moderate |
---
## Types of PNS X-Ray Scan
| View | What It Shows | When Used |
|------|---------------|-----------|
| \*\*Occipito-Mental (OM)\*\* | Maxillary sinuses & orbital floor | Routine screening |
| \*\*Lateral\*\* | Frontal & sphenoid sinus depth | Suspected frontal sinusitis |
| \*\*Water’s\*\* | Frontal & maxillary sinuses while patient’s mouth is open | Evaluation for sinus fluid levels |
---

## When and Who Needs to Take a PNS X-Ray Scan?

### Common Symptoms
- Chronic \*\*nasal congestion\*\* or blockage
- Persistent \*\*facial pain\*\* or pressure
- Recurrent \*\*headaches\*\* around the forehead or cheeks
- Thick nasal discharge lasting >10 days
### Referral Sources
- ENT specialists
- General practitioners
- Pulmonologists evaluating upper-airway involvement
---

## Risks & Limitations of the PNS X-Ray Scan

- \*\*Radiation exposure\*\*: ~0.03 mSv—equal to 3 days of natural background; well below annual safety limits (1 mSv).
- \*\*Limitations\*\*: Fine mucosal changes, small polyps or fungal balls may need a CT scan for confirmation.
---

## List of Parameters Considered During the PNS X-Ray Scan

- \*\*Sinus air-fluid levels\*\*
- \*\*Mucosal thickening\*\* (>4 mm is significant)
- \*\*Opacity or clouding\*\* of normally air-filled cavities
- \*\*Septal deviation\*\* and bony spur formation
---

## Reasons for Taking a PNS X-Ray Scan

- Confirm \*\*sinusitis severity\*\* before starting antibiotics
- Detect \*\*nasal polyps\*\* or \*\*sinonasal tumors\*\*
- \*\*Pre-surgical mapping\*\* for FESS (functional endoscopic sinus surgery)
---

## Benefits of Taking the PNS X-Ray Scan at Cadabams Diagnostics

- \*\*Digital imaging\*\* reduces dose by ~70 % versus film.
- \*\*Same-day reporting\*\* with colour-coded diagrams.
- \*\*Walk-in slots\*\* from 8 AM–8 PM, including Sundays.
- SMS link to download your report—no extra trip.
---

## Illnesses Diagnosed with PNS X-Ray Scan

- \*\*Acute & chronic sinusitis\*\*
- \*\*Nasal polyposis\*\*
- \*\*Fungal sinus infections\*\* (aspergillosis, mucormycosis)
- \*\*Sinonasal tumors\*\* (benign or malignant)
---

## Preparing for PNS X-Ray Scan

- Remove all metal: earrings, chains, hairpins, spectacles.
- Inform the technologist if you are \*\*pregnant\*\* or have \*\*removable dental implants\*\*.
- No fasting required.
---
## Pre-requisites PNS X-Ray Scan
- Valid \*\*doctor’s prescription\*\* (digital PDF accepted).
- Government-issued \*\*ID proof\*\* for registration.
---
## Best Time to Take the PNS X-Ray Scan
- \*\*Early morning\*\* if a contrast CT may follow (empty stomach).
- \*\*Avoid during active nosebleeds\*\* to prevent motion artefacts.
---
## Eligibility
- Adults and children \*\*≥5 years\*\*.
- \*\*Pregnancy screening\*\* performed for females aged 12–50.
---
## Procedure for Taking a PNS X-Ray Scan at Cadabams Diagnostics
1. \*\*Registration & consent\*\*: Fill a one-minute digital form.
2. \*\*Positioning\*\*: Stand upright; chin rests on the detector.
3. \*\*Image capture\*\*: 2–3 minutes total; you’ll hear a soft beep.
4. \*\*Digital report\*\*: Auto-generated and sent via SMS link within 2 hours.
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- Declare \*\*pregnancy status\*\*.
- Remove \*\*all head and neck jewelry\*\*.
- Stay \*\*absolutely still\*\* during exposure to avoid repeat shots.
---
